page: Microsoft Azure D64als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dalv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

The server is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This allocation represents a specific hardware tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute density over memory capacity. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64als_v6 demonstrates strong compute capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores rank in the top tier, placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core stress-ng performance, PassMark CPU scores, and software compilation speeds fall within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show strong write performance for large blocks, though read-only large block bandwidth is average. For workloads requiring high instruction throughput, the hardware profile offers high resource density, making it a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand large memory footprints.

Given its high core count, fast clock speed, and limited memory ratio, this server is suited for CPU-bound workloads. These include software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, image processing, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference tasks. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching due to the 2.0 GB per core memory limitation.
description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration results in a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks place its single-core stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench performance in the top 10%, with strong multi-threaded decompression and image processing capabilities. This resource profile offers high compute density, making it cost-efficient for CPU-bound workloads. It is suited for software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference, though the low memory ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v6 is a compute-optimized VM with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier CPU performance for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC vCPUs and an optimized two gigabytes of memory per core ratio.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs.
Features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core.
Lacks local storage but supports premium external storage options.
Top-tier single-core CPU and multi-core Geekbench benchmark performance.
Suited for software compilation, image processing, and static web serving.
